summary: Updated the repository configuration script so `--max-errors` defaults to 1 instead of 0, causing compiler invocation to stop after one error by default rather than allowing unlimited errors. The PR also cleaned up now-redundant `None` guards for argparse options with defaults and corrected/helpfully clarified related CLI help text for `--use-asm` and `--msg-style`.
what_changed: Compiler flags now always append `-maxerrors {args.max_errors}` because `argparse` supplies a default value.
`-nofail` is still appended only when `args.max_errors == 0`, preserving the explicit unlimited-errors behavior when the user asks for zero.
Removed conditional `is not None` checks around `args.max_errors`, `args.msg_style`, and `args.warn`, making flag generation unconditional for these defaulted arguments.
`--msg-style` is now always applied to both compiler flags and linker flags via `-msgstyle {args.msg_style}`.
`--warn` is now always emitted as `-warn {args.warn}` when building `cflags_base`.

Corrected the `--use-asm` help string from "don't incorporate .s files from asm directory" to "incorporate .s files from asm directory", matching the option name despite its internal `dest='no_asm'` with `store_false`.
Clarified `--msg-style` help text to say it controls the message style of the compiler and linker.
smart_moves: Kept `--max-errors 0` as the opt-in unlimited-errors mode by retaining the `args.max_errors == 0` branch that adds `-nofail`.
Simplified build flag assembly by relying on argparse defaults instead of defensive `None` checks for options that are always populated.
Updated help text near the behavior change, reducing future confusion for developers using `configure.py --help`.
Applied `msg_style` consistently to both compile and link flags, matching the clarified help text.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation build tooling, fail-fast compiler defaults can improve iteration by surfacing the first actionable error instead of flooding output with unlimited diagnostics.
If an argparse option has a real default, downstream build-flag code can usually treat it as non-null; removing unnecessary `None` checks makes configuration behavior easier to audit.
Preserve explicit legacy behavior through command-line overrides when changing defaults; here, users can still request unlimited errors with `--max-errors 0`.
CLI help strings should describe user-facing option behavior, not internal variable polarity such as `dest='no_asm'` combined with `store_false`.
naming_conventions: `--max-errors` maps to `args.max_errors` and emits the compiler flag `-maxerrors N`.
`--msg-style` maps to `args.msg_style` and emits `-msgstyle STYLE` for both compiler and linker flags.
`--use-asm` uses internal `dest='no_asm'` with `action='store_false'`; help text should still be phrased positively for the `--use-asm` option.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching or function decompilation tactic was present; this PR affected build configuration defaults.
The `--use-asm` help text touched the project behavior around incorporating `.s` files from the `asm` directory, but the diff did not change assembly inclusion logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were included in the PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that adjust `configure.py --max-errors` or `-maxerrors` behavior.
Search for build tooling changes that remove argparse `None` guards after adding defaults.
Search for PRs touching `--use-asm`, `no_asm`, or `.s` file incorporation from the asm directory.
Look for later review discussion about whether fail-fast compiler errors improved decompilation workflow.
